MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar May-09-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS

Plaintiff Bank of America N.A.'s motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted. The court may decide a motion for judgment on the pleadings on the face of the complaint and matters judicially noticeable, including judicial admissions. (Pang v. Beverly Hosp., Inc. (2000) 79 Cal.App.4th 986, 989-990.) The court grants the request for judicial notice.

In the complaint, Plaintiff pleaded an open book account for money due claim in the amount of $6,229.53. Defendant has admitted a balance owed of greater than the amount pleaded. (Ex. 1 to Langedyk Dec.) The court accordingly grants the motion. Plaintiff may submit a proposed judgment for the amount pleaded of $6,229.53 plus the amount in its cost bill of $418.61.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-05-2025 Set For Trial On Jun-30-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Deemed Admitted; Memorandum In Support Of Motion; Declaration In Support Of Motion
Off Calendar Feb-04-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'s Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Deemed Admitted.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Bank of America N.A.'s unopposed motion to deem requests for admissions admitted is granted. The truth of any matters specified in the requests is deemed admitted in light of responding party's failure to respond to the requests for admissions before the date of hearing. See CCP 2033.280(b). CCP 2033.280(c) states that a court order for sanctions is mandatory in this circumstance, although Bank of America has not sought sanctions. The court orders defendant Luis M. Arreola Garcia to pay $50 to Bank of America within 20 court days of the date of notice of entry of this order. Bank of America may if it chooses waive payment of the sanctions.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
